title: Connect & Analyze - Knowledge Check
description: Confirm you understand dtwiz credentials and system analysis.

questions.title: Passing the Classic access token
questions.content: The lab injects a Classic API token (dt0c01) as DT_OPERATOR_TOKEN. How does dtwiz accept it?

options.explanation: dtwiz deliberately does NOT read a Classic token from environment variables, so you always know which credential is used.

options.text: Automatically from the DT_ACCESS_TOKEN environment variable
options.explanation: dtwiz intentionally does not read Classic tokens from the environment - only DT_PLATFORM_TOKEN is read automatically.

options.text: It rewrites the token into a platform token first
options.explanation: Token types cannot be converted - they are different credentials.

questions.explanation: Classic tokens are opt-in: `dtwiz status --access-token "$DT_OPERATOR_TOKEN"`. Platform tokens (dt0s16) are read from DT_PLATFORM_TOKEN automatically.
hints: Check `dtwiz --help` - the flag description spells it out.

questions.title: What does `dtwiz analyze` need?
questions.content: Which credentials does `dtwiz analyze` require to inspect the local system?

options.text: None - it inspects the local machine only and sends nothing anywhere
options.explanation: analyze is a local inventory: platform, Docker, Kubernetes, cloud CLIs, agents, services.

options.text: A platform token, because it queries Grail
options.explanation: That's `dtwiz watch`. analyze is purely local.

options.text: kubectl access to a running cluster
options.explanation: A cluster is one thing analyze can DETECT, not a requirement.

questions.explanation: analyze runs offline against the local system - tokens only matter when dtwiz talks to your tenant (status, recommend, install, watch).
hints: You ran it before configuring anything tenant-side.

questions.title: Machine-readable analysis
questions.content: You want to feed the analysis into a script. Which command fits?

options.explanation: Both analyze and recommend support --json for structured output.
--verbose adds detail for humans, not structure for machines.

questions.explanation: `--json` turns the analyze/recommend output into structured JSON for automation.

learningObjectives: Run and read a system analysis.
